sections.heading: Complications
sections.content: Disease-related complications: Metastatic disease: Systemic therapy with TKIs; surgery or interventional procedures for limited disease [GIST-4, GIST-5]. Liver and peritoneal metastases are most common; lymph node metastases are rare except in SDH-deficient GIST [MS-2]. Tumor rupture or hemorrhage: Emergency surgery; considered as metastatic disease due to high recurrence risk [GIST-3, GIST-C]. Tumor rupture is a poor prognostic factor [v,52]. GI obstruction or perforation: Surgical intervention [GIST-C]. May present as acute abdomen requiring immediate attention [MS-2]. Treatment-related complications: Chemotherapy: GIST are generally resistant to conventional chemotherapy; no standard regimens are recommended [MS-2]. Immunotherapy: Limited data; ipilimumab plus nivolumab is considered for tumor mutational burden-high (TMB-H) GIST [27]. Targeted therapy: Imatinib: Common adverse events include fluid retention, diarrhea, nausea, fatigue, muscle cramps, abdominal pain, and rash. Serious toxicities: liver function test abnormalities (<5%), lung toxicity, neutropenia, GI bleeding, and cardiotoxicity (grade 3/4 in 8.2%) [59,60]. Management involves dose adjustments and supportive care. Sunitinib: Common toxicities: fatigue, nausea, vomiting, hematologic toxicities, diarrhea, hand-foot skin reaction (HFSR), hypertension, hypothyroidism, and cardiotoxicity. HFSR occurs in up to 20% of patients [65]; hypertension in 28% and hypothyroidism in 12% [68]. Management includes dose interruptions/reductions, emollients for HFSR, antihypertensives, and thyroid hormone replacement. Regorafenib: Similar TKI-related toxicities, with diarrhea, HFSR, and hypertension being common [88]. Ripretinib: Common adverse events include alopecia, diarrhea, nausea, and fatigue; serious potential for secondary malignancies [117]. Surgery: Risks include tumor rupture, spillage, bleeding, infection, and anastomotic leak. Avoidance of pseudocapsule violation is critical. Minimally invasive approaches are selected for favorable locations [GIST-C, MS-4]. Radiation: Palliative radiotherapy for symptomatic lesions, especially bone metastases; category 2B recommendation [GIST-5]. May cause local tissue damage.

cards.name: Metastatic disease
cards.detail: Systemic therapy with TKIs; surgery or interventional procedures for limited disease [GIST-4, GIST-5]. Liver and peritoneal metastases are most common; lymph node metastases are rare except in SDH-deficient GIST [MS-2].

cards.name: Tumor rupture or hemorrhage
cards.detail: Emergency surgery; considered as metastatic disease due to high recurrence risk [GIST-3, GIST-C]. Tumor rupture is a poor prognostic factor [v,52].

cards.name: GI obstruction or perforation
cards.detail: Surgical intervention [GIST-C]. May present as acute abdomen requiring immediate attention [MS-2].

cards.name: Liver function test abnormalities (imatinib)
cards.detail: Liver function test abnormalities (<5%) [59,60]. Management involves dose adjustments and supportive care.

cards.name: Cardiotoxicity (imatinib)
cards.detail: Cardiotoxicity (grade 3/4 in 8.2%) [59,60]. Management involves dose adjustments and supportive care.

cards.name: Hand-foot skin reaction (sunitinib)
cards.detail: Hand-foot skin reaction occurs in up to 20% of patients [65]. Management includes dose interruptions/reductions, emollients for HFSR.

cards.name: Hypothyroidism (sunitinib)
cards.detail: Hypothyroidism in 12% [68]. Management includes thyroid hormone replacement.

cards.name: Surgery complications
cards.detail: Risks include tumor rupture, spillage, bleeding, infection, and anastomotic leak. Avoidance of pseudocapsule violation is critical.

cards.name: Radiation local tissue damage
cards.detail: Palliative radiotherapy for symptomatic lesions, especially bone metastases; category 2B recommendation [GIST-5]. May cause local tissue damage.

cards.name: Common adverse events (imatinib)
cards.detail: Fluid retention, diarrhea, nausea, fatigue, muscle cramps, abdominal pain, and rash.

cards.name: Common toxicities (sunitinib)
cards.detail: Fatigue, nausea, vomiting, hematologic toxicities, diarrhea, cardiotoxicity.
